zh.Mabe, Cornwall, England: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾馬貝
zh.Fentongollan,Tregothnan Cornwall, Englan: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾特雷格南
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Basset於大約1372年7月2日出生在英國可瓦爾時，她的父親William de Basset III35歲，母親Margaret Fleming26歲。她於大約1400年與Ralph Trenoweth結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在英國薩默塞特。

zh-hans.Mabe, Cornwall, England: 英国英格兰可瓦爾馬貝
zh-hans.Fentongollan,Tregothnan Cornwall, Englan: 英国英格兰可瓦爾特雷格南
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Basset于大约1372年7月2日出生在英国可瓦爾时，她的父亲William de Basset III35岁，母亲Margaret Fleming26岁。她于大约1400年与Ralph Trenoweth结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在英国薩默塞特。

nameMeanings.name: Basset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from a diminutive of Old French bas(se) ‘short, of low stature’ (see Bas ).
English: variant of   Bassett  , a cognate of 1 above.
In some cases also an altered form of French Besset (see Bessette 2). Compare Bassette 1.

variants.name: Bas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Mauricio, Abelardo, Florencio, Francisco, Jorge, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Soledad, Vicente.  Eastern Slavic Dmytro, Liya, Pyotr, Sergey.
Catalan: habitational name from a place so named in the district of La Garrotxa, Catalonia.
Basque: topographic name for someone who lived by a wood, from baso ‘wood, forest’.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Basset was born about 2 July 1372, in Cornwall, England, her father, William de Basset III, was 35 and her mother, Margaret Fleming, was 26. She married Ralph Trenoweth about 1400. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Basset est née environ 2 juillet 1372, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, William de Basset III, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Margaret Fleming, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph Trenoweth environ 1400. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Basset um 2. Juli 1372 in Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Basset III 35 und ihre Mutter Margaret Fleming 26. Sie hat um 1400 Ralph Trenoweth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Basset è nata il circa 2 luglio 1372 nel Cornwall, England, sua padre, William de Basset III, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Margaret Fleming, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Ralph Trenoweth circa 1400. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Bassetが約1372年7月2日にイギリスコルンワルで生まれた時，父William de Basset IIIは35歳，母Margaret Flemingは26歳であった。約1400年にRalph Trenowethと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスソメルセトで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Basset(이)가 대략 1372년 7월 2일 영국 코른월에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Basset III(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Fleming(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1400년 Ralph Trenoweth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 서머세트에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Basset nasceu aproximadamente 2 de julho de 1372, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Basset III, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Fleming, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Ralph Trenoweth aproximadamente 1400. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Basset родилась около 2 июля 1372 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Basset III было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Fleming, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph Trenoweth около 1400 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Basset nació aproximadamente 2 de julio de 1372, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Basset III, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Margaret Fleming, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Ralph Trenoweth aproximadamente 1400. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en Somerset, Inglaterra.
